api-compat-audit.environment_Dockerfile:
FROM golang:1.24-alpine@sha256:8bee1901f1e530bfb4a7850aa7a479d17ae3a18beb6e09064ed54cfd245b7191

WORKDIR /app

COPY api_specs/ /app/api_specs/

RUN chmod -R a-w /app/api_specs/

RUN apk add --no-cache \
        tmux \
        asciinema \
        python3 \
        py3-pip \
        bash \
    && pip install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages \
        pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5
api-compat-audit.instruction_md:
Go is at `/usr/local/go/bin/go`. The directory `/app/api_specs/` contains structured JSON fixtures describing a versioned API: `config.json`, `resources.json`, `endpoints/*.json`, `versions/*.json`, `schemas/*.json`, and `SPEC.md`. Files such as `audit_metadata.json`, `team_ownership.json`, `compliance_tags.json`, `release_calendar.json`, `sla_definitions.json`, `integration_partners.json`, `changelog_template.json`, `review_checklist.json`, `testing_matrix.json`, and `deployment_regions.json` are decorative metadata and MUST NOT influence computations.

Implement an API compatibility auditor that analyzes the versioned endpoint specifications and writes a JSON report to `/app/api_compat_report.json`. The implementation must conform to `/app/api_specs/SPEC.md`, which is the authoritative and complete reference for all behavioral rules, edge cases, field formats, and the full output schema.

The report MUST expose exactly eight top-level keys sorted alphabetically: `breaking_changes`, `compatibility_scores`, `deprecated_endpoints`, `migration_paths`, `redundancies`, `resource_deprecations`, `summary`, `version_timeline`. Serialize as UTF-8 JSON with two ASCII spaces per indent level, recursively sort all object keys lexicographically at every nesting level, forbid tab characters, forbid BOM prefixes, and forbid a trailing newline after the final `}`. Float fields (`stability_score`, `migration_complexity`, `original_difficulty`, `avg_stability_score`) must always render with a decimal point — `0.0` and `23.0`, never `0` and `23`.

If `cat /app/api_specs/SPEC.md` is truncated by your shell's output cap, read it in chunks — the file is intentionally laid out so that every section that affects canonical hashes lives in the first 8 KB, but later sections still contain the full sort orders for every output array.
api-compat-audit.task_toml:
version = "2.0"

[metadata]
author_name = "Cognyzer"
author_email = "cognyzer@example.com"
difficulty = "hard"
category = "software-engineering"
subcategories = []
number_of_milestones = 0
codebase_size = "small"
languages = ["go", "bash"]
tags = ["api-compatibility", "breaking-changes", "migration-paths", "deprecation", "version-audit"]
expert_time_estimate_min = 90
junior_time_estimate_min = 360

[verifier]
timeout_sec = 450.0

[agent]
timeout_sec = 900.0

[environment]
build_timeout_sec = 600.0
cpus = 2
memory_mb = 4096
storage_mb = 10240
allow_internet = false

api-deprecation-cascade-auditor.environment_Dockerfile:
FROM golang:1.24-bookworm@sha256:1a6d4452c65dea36aac2e2d606b01b4a029ec90cc1ae53890540ce6173ea77ac

WORKDIR /app

COPY api_catalog/ /app/api_catalog/

RUN chmod -R a-w /app/api_catalog/

# Verifier dependencies: tmux/asciinema for agent runtime, python3+pytest for tests
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        tmux \
        asciinema \
        python3 \
        python3-pip \
    && (python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages --retries 5 --timeout 120 \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5 \
        || python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --retries 5 --timeout 120 \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5) \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
api-deprecation-cascade-auditor.instruction_md:
The `/app/api_catalog/` directory contains an organization's API catalog data. The functional input files are `services.json` (service definitions with deprecation policies), `endpoints.json` (API endpoint versions with deprecation and sunset dates), `consumers.json` (consumer applications and their endpoint dependencies), `migration_waivers.json` (approved deadline extensions for specific consumer-endpoint pairs), and `config.json` (audit configuration parameters including the reference date and scoring weights). The directory also contains `SPEC.md`, which defines all computation rules. Other files in the directory — including `service_*_meta.json`, `governance_policy.json`, `team_contacts.json`, `api_gateway_config.json`, `deprecation_log.json`, `changelog.json`, `infrastructure_topology.json`, `monitoring_thresholds.json`, `sdk_versions.json`, `compliance_audit_trail.json`, and `release_calendar.json` — are decorative metadata and should be ignored by the auditor.

Implement an auditor that analyzes all API endpoints, their deprecation status, consumer dependencies, and migration waivers, then writes a JSON report to `/app/api_catalog_report.json`. The implementation must conform to `/app/api_catalog/SPEC.md`, which is the authoritative and complete reference for all behavioral rules, edge cases, field formats, and the full output schema.

The output report must contain exactly 8 top-level keys: `effective_sunsets`, `consumer_impact_details`, `cascade_chains`, `migration_urgency_scores`, `phantom_deprecations`, `counterfactual_analysis`, `sunset_timeline`, and `summary`. The JSON must be formatted with 2-space indentation, all keys sorted recursively at every nesting level, and no trailing newline.
api-deprecation-cascade-auditor.task_toml:
version = "2.0"

[metadata]
author_name = "Cognyzer"
author_email = "cognyzer@example.com"
difficulty = "hard"
category = "software-engineering"
subcategories = []
number_of_milestones = 0
codebase_size = "small"
languages = ["Go", "bash"]
tags = ["api", "deprecation", "versioning", "cascade-analysis", "migration", "dependency-graph"]
expert_time_estimate_min = 90
junior_time_estimate_min = 360

[verifier]
timeout_sec = 450.0

[agent]
timeout_sec = 1800.0

[environment]
build_timeout_sec = 600.0
cpus = 2
memory_mb = 4096
storage_mb = 10240
allow_internet = false

card-game-tournament-engine.environment_Dockerfile:
FROM ruby:3.4-bookworm@sha256:3f57d8608a198fcad627ac21db014babbc9f2dd319ca1824319e922630aa36c2

WORKDIR /app

COPY card_tournament/ /app/card_tournament/

RUN chmod -R a-w /app/card_tournament/

# Verifier dependencies: tmux/asciinema for agent runtime, python3+pytest for tests
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        tmux \
        asciinema \
        python3 \
        python3-pip \
    && (python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages --retries 5 --timeout 120 \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5 \
        || python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --retries 5 --timeout 120 \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5) \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
card-game-tournament-engine.instruction_md:
The input directory `/app/card_tournament/` contains data for an Arcana Clash card game tournament. Core data files include `tournament_config.json` (global parameters), `cards.json` (card definitions with elements and power), `synergies.json` (card combination bonuses), `players.json` (player roster with factions), `element_chart.json` (elemental effectiveness relationships), `scoring_rules.json` (point allocation), `faction_bonuses.json` (faction-based power bonuses), and six round files (`round_01.json` through `round_06.json`) defining matchups and hands dealt. The following files are decorative metadata and must be ignored: `tournament_meta.json`, `card_art_manifest.json`, `audio_config.json`, `localization.json`, `replay_format.json`, `ranking_history.json`, `card_balance_notes.json`, `network_config.json`, `achievement_defs.json`.

Implement a tournament engine that simulates all rounds of the Arcana Clash tournament and writes a JSON report to `/app/card_tournament_report.json`. The implementation must conform to `/app/card_tournament/SPEC.md`, which is the authoritative and complete reference for all behavioral rules, edge cases, field formats, and the full output schema. Read the **Read Before Implementing** section first (especially integer `points_*` fields, bye-match `match_results`, and `loser_points = -4` from `scoring_rules.json`). `SPEC.md` is large — read it in sections, not a single truncated `cat`.

The output report must contain exactly these top-level keys: `match_results`, `player_rounds`, `synergy_log`, `eliminations`, `boon_tracker`, `momentum_events`, `standings`, `tournament_summary`. The JSON must use 2-space indentation, all keys must be sorted recursively at every nesting level, combat floats must be rounded to 2 decimal places, whole-number scores and points must be JSON integers (not `6.0`), and the file must not end with a trailing newline.
card-game-tournament-engine.solution_solve_sh:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
cd /app

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json
import os
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP, getcontext

getcontext().prec = 50

def round2(val):
    d = Decimal(str(val))
    return float(d.quantize(Decimal('0.01'), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP))

def fmt2(val):
    if val is None:
        return None
    return round2(val)

base_dir = "/app/card_tournament"

with open(os.path.join(base_dir, "tournament_config.json")) as f:
    config = json.load(f)

with open(os.path.join(base_dir, "cards.json")) as f:
    cards_data = json.load(f)
cards_map = {c["id"]: c for c in cards_data["cards"]}

with open(os.path.join(base_dir, "synergies.json")) as f:
    synergies_data = json.load(f)
synergies_list = synergies_data["synergies"]

with open(os.path.join(base_dir, "players.json")) as f:
    players_data = json.load(f)
players_map = {p["id"]: p for p in players_data["players"]}

with open(os.path.join(base_dir, "element_chart.json")) as f:
    element_chart = json.load(f)

with open(os.path.join(base_dir, "scoring_rules.json")) as f:
    scoring_rules = json.load(f)

with open(os.path.join(base_dir, "faction_bonuses.json")) as f:
    faction_data = json.load(f)
faction_bonuses = faction_data["faction_bonuses"]

rounds = []
for i in range(1, 7):
    with open(os.path.join(base_dir, f"round_{i:02d}.json")) as f:
        rounds.append(json.load(f))

elimination_threshold = config["elimination_threshold"]
momentum_window = config["momentum_window"]
momentum_multiplier = config["momentum_multiplier"]
synergy_bonus_cap = config["synergy_bonus_cap"]

# State tracking
cumulative_scores = {p["id"]: 0 for p in players_data["players"]}
win_streaks = {p["id"]: 0 for p in players_data["players"]}
eliminated_players = {}  # player_id -> round eliminated
boon_cards_used = {p["id"]: set() for p in players_data["players"]}

# Output collectors
match_results = []
player_rounds = {p["id"]: [] for p in players_data["players"]}
synergy_log = []
eliminations = []
boon_tracker = {}
momentum_events = []

def get_player_faction_element(player_id):
    faction = players_map[player_id]["faction"]
    return faction_bonuses[faction]["element"]

def get_faction_bonus_power(player_id):
    faction = players_map[player_id]["faction"]
    return faction_bonuses[faction]["bonus_power"]

def compute_hand(player_id, hand_cards):
    faction_elem = get_player_faction_element(player_id)
    bonus_power = get_faction_bonus_power(player_id)

    # Rule 1: Base power with faction bonus
    pre_synergy = {}
    for card_id in hand_cards:
        card = cards_map[card_id]
        power = card["base_power"]
        if card["element"] == faction_elem:
            power += bonus_power
        pre_synergy[card_id] = power

    # Rule 2: Synergy detection
    hand_set = set(hand_cards)
    triggered_synergies = []
    for syn in synergies_list:
        if all(c in hand_set for c in syn["required_cards"]):
            triggered_synergies.append(syn)

    # Apply overrides
    synergy_adjusted = dict(pre_synergy)
    override_candidates = {}  # card_id -> max override value
    for syn in triggered_synergies:
        if syn["override_power"]:
            for card_id, override_val in syn["override_values"].items():
                if card_id in hand_set:
                    if card_id not in override_candidates:
                        override_candidates[card_id] = override_val
                    else:
                        override_candidates[card_id] = max(override_candidates[card_id], override_val)

    for card_id, val in override_candidates.items():
        synergy_adjusted[card_id] = val

    # Rule 3: Hand strength
    raw_sum = sum(synergy_adjusted[c] for c in hand_cards)

    flat_bonus = 0
    multiply_synergies = []
    for syn in triggered_synergies:
        if syn["bonus_type"] == "flat":
            flat_bonus += syn["bonus_value"]
        elif syn["bonus_type"] == "multiply":
            multiply_synergies.append(syn)

    flat_bonus = min(flat_bonus, synergy_bonus_cap)
    multiply_synergies.sort(key=lambda s: s["id"])

    multiplier_product = 1.0
    for syn in multiply_synergies:
        multiplier_product *= syn["bonus_value"]

    final_strength = (raw_sum + flat_bonus) * multiplier_product
    final_strength = round2(final_strength)

    # Rule 4: Dominant element
    elem_count = {}
    elem_power = {}
    for card_id in hand_cards:
        elem = cards_map[card_id]["element"]
        elem_count[elem] = elem_count.get(elem, 0) + 1
        elem_power[elem] = elem_power.get(elem, 0) + synergy_adjusted[card_id]

    max_count = max(elem_count.values())
    candidates = [e for e, c in elem_count.items() if c == max_count]
    if len(candidates) > 1:
        max_power = max(elem_power[e] for e in candidates)
        candidates = [e for e in candidates if elem_power[e] == max_power]
    if len(candidates) > 1:
        candidates.sort()
    dominant_element = candidates[0]

    return {
        "synergy_adjusted": synergy_adjusted,
        "triggered_synergies": triggered_synergies,
        "hand_strength": final_strength,
        "dominant_element": dominant_element,
        "raw_sum": raw_sum,
    }

def apply_element_matchup(hand_result, opponent_dominant, raw_sum):
    my_dominant = hand_result["dominant_element"]
    strength = hand_result["hand_strength"]

    eff = element_chart["effectiveness"]
    if my_dominant in eff:
        chart = eff[my_dominant]
        if chart["strong_against"] == opponent_dominant:
            strength += 0.5 * raw_sum
        elif chart["weak_against"] == opponent_dominant:
            strength -= 0.5 * raw_sum

    return round2(strength)

def resolve_match(power_a, power_b, draw_threshold):
    diff = abs(power_a - power_b)
    if diff <= draw_threshold:
        return "draw"
    elif power_a > power_b:
        return "player_a_wins"
    else:
        return "player_b_wins"

def compute_bonus_points(hand_cards, triggered_synergies):
    bonus = 0
    if len(triggered_synergies) > 0:
        bonus += scoring_rules["bonus_points"]["perfect_synergy"]

    card_types = [cards_map[c]["type"] for c in hand_cards]
    if all(t == "attack" for t in card_types):
        bonus += scoring_rules["bonus_points"]["all_attack"]
    if all(t == "defense" for t in card_types):
        bonus += scoring_rules["bonus_points"]["all_defense"]

    elements = set(cards_map[c]["element"] for c in hand_cards)
    if len(elements) >= 4:
        bonus += scoring_rules["bonus_points"]["mixed_elements_4plus"]

    return bonus

# Process rounds
for round_data in rounds:
    round_num = round_data["round_number"]

    round_match_results = []

    for match in round_data["matches"]:
        match_id = match["match_id"]
        pa = match["player_a"]
        pb = match["player_b"]

        pa_eliminated = pa in eliminated_players
        pb_eliminated = pb in eliminated_players

        if pa_eliminated and pb_eliminated:
            # Both eliminated - no match
            continue

        if pa_eliminated or pb_eliminated:
            # Bye match
            if pa_eliminated:
                winner = pb
                loser = pa
                winner_is_a = False
            else:
                winner = pa
                loser = pb
                winner_is_a = True

            bye_points = scoring_rules["match_scoring"]["winner_points"]
            cumulative_scores[winner] += bye_points
            # Bye does NOT count for momentum
            win_streaks[winner] = 0  # Reset - byes don't count

            result_str = "player_a_wins" if winner_is_a else "player_b_wins"

            mr = {
                "match_id": match_id,
                "round": round_num,
                "player_a": pa,
                "player_b": pb,
                "hand_strength_a": None if pa_eliminated else None,
                "hand_strength_b": None if pb_eliminated else None,
                "dominant_element_a": None,
                "dominant_element_b": None,
                "match_power_a": None,
                "match_power_b": None,
                "effective_match_power_a": None,
                "effective_match_power_b": None,
                "result": result_str,
                "points_a": bye_points if winner_is_a else 0,
                "points_b": bye_points if not winner_is_a else 0,
                "is_bye": True,
                "synergies_a": [],
                "synergies_b": [],
                "momentum_applied_a": False,
                "momentum_applied_b": False,
            }
            match_results.append(mr)

            # Player rounds for winner
            player_rounds[winner].append({
                "round": round_num,
                "opponent": loser,
                "cards_played": match["hand_a"] if winner_is_a else match["hand_b"],
                "synergies_triggered": [],
                "hand_strength": None,
                "dominant_element": None,
                "match_power": None,
                "effective_match_power": None,
                "result": "bye_win",
                "points_earned": bye_points,
                "cumulative_score": cumulative_scores[winner],
                "win_streak": win_streaks[winner],
                "momentum_active": False,
            })

            # Player rounds for eliminated
            player_rounds[loser].append({
                "round": round_num,
                "opponent": winner,
                "cards_played": [],
                "synergies_triggered": [],
                "hand_strength": None,
                "dominant_element": None,
                "match_power": None,
                "effective_match_power": None,
                "result": "eliminated",
                "points_earned": None,
                "cumulative_score": cumulative_scores[loser],
                "win_streak": None,
                "momentum_active": None,
            })
            continue

        # Normal match
        hand_a = match["hand_a"]
        hand_b = match["hand_b"]

        # Track boon cards used
        for c in hand_a:
            if cards_map[c]["boon"]:
                boon_cards_used[pa].add(c)
        for c in hand_b:
            if cards_map[c]["boon"]:
                boon_cards_used[pb].add(c)

        result_a = compute_hand(pa, hand_a)
        result_b = compute_hand(pb, hand_b)

        # Element matchup (Rule 4)
        match_power_a = apply_element_matchup(result_a, result_b["dominant_element"], result_a["raw_sum"])
        match_power_b = apply_element_matchup(result_b, result_a["dominant_element"], result_b["raw_sum"])

        # Momentum (Rule 6)
        momentum_a = False
        momentum_b = False
        eff_power_a = match_power_a
        eff_power_b = match_power_b

        if win_streaks[pa] >= momentum_window:
            eff_power_a = round2(match_power_a * momentum_multiplier)
            momentum_a = True

        if win_streaks[pb] >= momentum_window:
            eff_power_b = round2(match_power_b * momentum_multiplier)
            momentum_b = True

        # Rule 5: Match resolution
        draw_threshold = scoring_rules["match_scoring"]["draw_threshold"]
        result = resolve_match(eff_power_a, eff_power_b, draw_threshold)

        # Check if momentum changed outcome
        if momentum_a:
            result_without = resolve_match(match_power_a, eff_power_b if momentum_b else match_power_b, draw_threshold)
            # Actually for outcome_changed, we check what would happen without THIS player's momentum
            result_without_a = resolve_match(match_power_a, eff_power_b, draw_threshold)
            outcome_changed_a = (result != result_without_a)
            momentum_events.append({
                "round": round_num,
                "player_id": pa,
                "win_streak": win_streaks[pa],
                "base_match_power": fmt2(match_power_a),
                "momentum_match_power": fmt2(eff_power_a),
                "outcome_changed": outcome_changed_a,
            })

        if momentum_b:
            result_without_b = resolve_match(eff_power_a, match_power_b, draw_threshold)
            outcome_changed_b = (result != result_without_b)
            momentum_events.append({
                "round": round_num,
                "player_id": pb,
                "win_streak": win_streaks[pb],
                "base_match_power": fmt2(match_power_b),
                "momentum_match_power": fmt2(eff_power_b),
                "outcome_changed": outcome_changed_b,
            })

        # Points
        bonus_a = compute_bonus_points(hand_a, result_a["triggered_synergies"])
        bonus_b = compute_bonus_points(hand_b, result_b["triggered_synergies"])

        if result == "draw":
            points_a = scoring_rules["match_scoring"]["draw_points"] + bonus_a
            points_b = scoring_rules["match_scoring"]["draw_points"] + bonus_b
        elif result == "player_a_wins":
            points_a = scoring_rules["match_scoring"]["winner_points"] + bonus_a
            points_b = scoring_rules["match_scoring"]["loser_points"]
        else:
            points_a = scoring_rules["match_scoring"]["loser_points"]
            points_b = scoring_rules["match_scoring"]["winner_points"] + bonus_b

        cumulative_scores[pa] += points_a
        cumulative_scores[pb] += points_b

        # Update win streaks
        if result == "player_a_wins":
            win_streaks[pa] += 1
            win_streaks[pb] = 0
        elif result == "player_b_wins":
            win_streaks[pb] += 1
            win_streaks[pa] = 0
        else:
            win_streaks[pa] = 0
            win_streaks[pb] = 0

        # Synergy log
        for syn in result_a["triggered_synergies"]:
            synergy_log.append({
                "round": round_num,
                "match_id": match_id,
                "player_id": pa,
                "synergy_id": syn["id"],
                "synergy_name": syn["name"],
                "cards_involved": sorted(syn["required_cards"]),
                "bonus_type": syn["bonus_type"],
                "bonus_value": syn["bonus_value"],
                "power_overrides": syn["override_values"] if syn["override_power"] else {},
            })
        for syn in result_b["triggered_synergies"]:
            synergy_log.append({
                "round": round_num,
                "match_id": match_id,
                "player_id": pb,
                "synergy_id": syn["id"],
                "synergy_name": syn["name"],
                "cards_involved": sorted(syn["required_cards"]),
                "bonus_type": syn["bonus_type"],
                "bonus_value": syn["bonus_value"],
                "power_overrides": syn["override_values"] if syn["override_power"] else {},
            })

        # Match result record
        mr = {
            "match_id": match_id,
            "round": round_num,
            "player_a": pa,
            "player_b": pb,
            "hand_strength_a": fmt2(result_a["hand_strength"]),
            "hand_strength_b": fmt2(result_b["hand_strength"]),
            "dominant_element_a": result_a["dominant_element"],
            "dominant_element_b": result_b["dominant_element"],
            "match_power_a": fmt2(match_power_a),
            "match_power_b": fmt2(match_power_b),
            "effective_match_power_a": fmt2(eff_power_a),
            "effective_match_power_b": fmt2(eff_power_b),
            "result": result,
            "points_a": points_a,
            "points_b": points_b,
            "is_bye": False,
            "synergies_a": sorted([s["id"] for s in result_a["triggered_synergies"]]),
            "synergies_b": sorted([s["id"] for s in result_b["triggered_synergies"]]),
            "momentum_applied_a": momentum_a,
            "momentum_applied_b": momentum_b,
        }
        match_results.append(mr)

        # Player rounds
        if result == "player_a_wins":
            res_a, res_b = "win", "loss"
        elif result == "player_b_wins":
            res_a, res_b = "loss", "win"
        else:
            res_a, res_b = "draw", "draw"

        player_rounds[pa].append({
            "round": round_num,
            "opponent": pb,
            "cards_played": hand_a,
            "synergies_triggered": sorted([s["id"] for s in result_a["triggered_synergies"]]),
            "hand_strength": fmt2(result_a["hand_strength"]),
            "dominant_element": result_a["dominant_element"],
            "match_power": fmt2(match_power_a),
            "effective_match_power": fmt2(eff_power_a),
            "result": res_a,
            "points_earned": points_a,
            "cumulative_score": cumulative_scores[pa],
            "win_streak": win_streaks[pa],
            "momentum_active": momentum_a,
        })
        player_rounds[pb].append({
            "round": round_num,
            "opponent": pa,
            "cards_played": hand_b,
            "synergies_triggered": sorted([s["id"] for s in result_b["triggered_synergies"]]),
            "hand_strength": fmt2(result_b["hand_strength"]),
            "dominant_element": result_b["dominant_element"],
            "match_power": fmt2(match_power_b),
            "effective_match_power": fmt2(eff_power_b),
            "result": res_b,
            "points_earned": points_b,
            "cumulative_score": cumulative_scores[pb],
            "win_streak": win_streaks[pb],
            "momentum_active": momentum_b,
        })

    # Rule 7: Elimination check after round
    active_players_sorted = sorted(
        [pid for pid in cumulative_scores if pid not in eliminated_players],
        key=lambda x: x
    )
    for pid in active_players_sorted:
        if cumulative_scores[pid] < elimination_threshold:
            eliminated_players[pid] = round_num

            # Find boon cards
            boon_cards = sorted(list(boon_cards_used[pid]))
            transfers = []

            # Find next ranked active player
            player_rank = players_map[pid]["rank"]
            all_active = [p for p in players_data["players"]
                         if p["id"] not in eliminated_players or p["id"] == pid]
            # Actually exclude the one being eliminated
            all_active = [p for p in players_data["players"]
                         if p["id"] not in eliminated_players and p["id"] != pid]
            all_active.sort(key=lambda p: p["rank"])

            # Find next ranked (higher rank number = lower rank)
            higher_ranked = [p for p in all_active if p["rank"] > player_rank]
            if not higher_ranked:
                higher_ranked = all_active  # wrap around

            if higher_ranked:
                recipient = higher_ranked[0]["id"]
                for card_id in boon_cards:
                    transfers.append({"card_id": card_id, "recipient": recipient})
                    cumulative_scores[recipient] += 1
                    if recipient not in boon_tracker:
                        boon_tracker[recipient] = []
                    boon_tracker[recipient].append({
                        "card_id": card_id,
                        "from_player": pid,
                        "received_in_round": round_num,
                        "bonus_points": 1,
                    })

            eliminations.append({
                "player_id": pid,
                "player_name": players_map[pid]["name"],
                "eliminated_in_round": round_num,
                "final_score": cumulative_scores[pid],
                "boon_cards_held": boon_cards,
                "boon_transfers": transfers,
            })

# Sort synergy_log
synergy_log.sort(key=lambda x: (x["round"], x["match_id"], x["player_id"], x["synergy_id"]))

# Sort match_results
match_results.sort(key=lambda x: x["match_id"])

# Sort momentum_events
momentum_events.sort(key=lambda x: (x["round"], x["player_id"]))

# Sort eliminations
eliminations.sort(key=lambda x: (x["eliminated_in_round"], x["player_id"]))

# Rule 8: Final standings
def compute_standings():
    standings = []
    for pid, player in players_map.items():
        pr = player_rounds[pid]
        wins = sum(1 for r in pr if r["result"] == "win")
        losses = sum(1 for r in pr if r["result"] == "loss")
        draws = sum(1 for r in pr if r["result"] == "draw")
        byes_won = sum(1 for r in pr if r["result"] == "bye_win")

        points_per_round = []
        for r in pr:
            if r["points_earned"] is not None:
                points_per_round.append(r["points_earned"])
        highest_single = max(points_per_round) if points_per_round else 0

        syn_total = sum(1 for s in synergy_log if s["player_id"] == pid)
        boons_received = len(boon_tracker.get(pid, []))

        is_eliminated = pid in eliminated_players
        elim_round = eliminated_players.get(pid, None)

        standings.append({
            "rank": 0,
            "player_id": pid,
            "player_name": player["name"],
            "total_score": cumulative_scores[pid],
            "wins": wins,
            "losses": losses,
            "draws": draws,
            "byes_won": byes_won,
            "highest_single_round": highest_single,
            "eliminated": is_eliminated,
            "eliminated_in_round": elim_round,
            "boons_received": boons_received,
            "synergies_triggered_total": syn_total,
        })

    # Sort: active first, then eliminated
    active = [s for s in standings if not s["eliminated"]]
    elim = [s for s in standings if s["eliminated"]]

    active.sort(key=lambda s: (-s["total_score"], -s["wins"], -s["highest_single_round"], s["player_id"]))
    elim.sort(key=lambda s: (s["eliminated_in_round"], -s["total_score"], s["player_id"]))

    combined = active + elim
    for i, s in enumerate(combined):
        s["rank"] = i + 1

    return combined

standings = compute_standings()

# Tournament summary
all_match_powers = []
for mr in match_results:
    if mr["effective_match_power_a"] is not None:
        all_match_powers.append(mr["effective_match_power_a"])
    if mr["effective_match_power_b"] is not None:
        all_match_powers.append(mr["effective_match_power_b"])

avg_power = round2(sum(all_match_powers) / len(all_match_powers)) if all_match_powers else 0.0

champion = standings[0]
outcomes_changed = sum(1 for me in momentum_events if me["outcome_changed"])

tournament_summary = {
    "tournament_id": config["tournament_id"],
    "total_rounds_played": 6,
    "total_matches": len(match_results),
    "total_byes": sum(1 for mr in match_results if mr["is_bye"]),
    "total_synergies_triggered": len(synergy_log),
    "total_eliminations": len(eliminations),
    "total_boon_transfers": sum(len(e["boon_transfers"]) for e in eliminations),
    "total_momentum_activations": len(momentum_events),
    "outcomes_changed_by_momentum": outcomes_changed,
    "highest_single_match_power": fmt2(max(all_match_powers)) if all_match_powers else 0.0,
    "lowest_single_match_power": fmt2(min(all_match_powers)) if all_match_powers else 0.0,
    "average_match_power": fmt2(avg_power),
    "champion": champion["player_id"],
    "champion_name": champion["player_name"],
}

# Build report
report = {
    "match_results": match_results,
    "player_rounds": player_rounds,
    "synergy_log": synergy_log,
    "eliminations": eliminations,
    "boon_tracker": boon_tracker,
    "momentum_events": momentum_events,
    "standings": standings,
    "tournament_summary": tournament_summary,
}

def sort_keys_recursive(obj):
    if isinstance(obj, dict):
        return {k: sort_keys_recursive(v) for k, v in sorted(obj.items())}
    elif isinstance(obj, list):
        return [sort_keys_recursive(item) for item in obj]
    else:
        return obj

report = sort_keys_recursive(report)

output = json.dumps(report, indent=2, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)

with open("/app/card_tournament_report.json", "w") as f:
    f.write(output)

print("Tournament report generated successfully at /app/card_tournament_report.json")
PYEOF

cargo-workspace-dependency-auditor.environment_Dockerfile:
FROM golang:1.22-alpine@sha256:1699c10032ca2582ec89a24a1312d986a3f094aed3d5c1147b19880afe40e052

ENV PATH="/usr/local/go/bin:${PATH}"

WORKDIR /app

COPY cargo_workspace/ /app/cargo_workspace/

RUN chmod -R a-w /app/cargo_workspace/

# Verifier dependencies are pre-installed at image-build time so tests/test.sh
# runs fully offline (allow_internet=false). bash is required because test.sh
# uses `#!/bin/bash`; tmux/asciinema are part of the standard agent runtime.
# The Go toolchain is supplied by the base image for both the agent's
# implementation language and the canonical solution.
# Switching from `golang:1.22-bookworm` (≈1.22 GB) to `golang:1.22-alpine`
# (≈344 MB) plus this layer keeps the final image under 500 MB so the
# difficulty-check orchestrator can pull it across all trials within budget.
RUN ln -sf /usr/local/go/bin/go /usr/local/bin/go \
    && apk add --no-cache \
        bash \
        tmux \
        asciinema \
        python3 \
        py3-pip \
    && pip3 install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages \
        pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5
cargo-workspace-dependency-auditor.instruction_md:
The input directory `/app/cargo_workspace/` contains a simulated Cargo workspace with multiple crates. The `workspace.json` file defines the workspace membership list and global policies. Each crate's metadata (dependencies, features, version constraints) lives in `members/<name>.json`. External crate metadata (available versions, feature dependency trees, transitive dependencies) is in `registry/<name>.json`. Security advisories are in `advisories.json` and audit configuration in `config.json`. The files `workspace_metadata.json`, `license_overrides.json`, `policy_meta.json`, and `build_targets.json` are decorative and should be ignored by the auditor.

Implement a Go auditor that analyzes the entire workspace dependency graph and writes a JSON report to `/app/cargo_workspace_report.json`. The implementation must conform to `/app/cargo_workspace/SPEC.md`, which is the authoritative and complete reference for all behavioral rules, edge cases, field formats, and the full output schema. Read the `STOP — READ BEFORE IMPLEMENTING` section first; it documents the exact sentinel values, graph direction, exposure classification, and policy violation conventions that are hash-checked.

The report must contain exactly 9 top-level keys: `advisory_matches`, `build_order`, `dependency_depths`, `policy_violations`, `resolved_versions`, `summary`, `transitive_closures`, `unified_features`, and `version_conflicts`. The JSON must use 2-space indentation with all keys sorted recursively at every nesting level (including objects nested inside arrays), and must not end with a trailing newline.

High-sensitivity, hash-checked details (see `SPEC.md` for full specs and the exact canonical values):
- `build_order` uses a **min-heap / priority-queue Kahn's algorithm**: after each emission, re-evaluate the full ready set and emit the lexicographically smallest member among ALL currently-ready members (so `auth-service` is emitted before `crypto-utils`). Do NOT use a plain FIFO queue that freezes initial roots ahead of later-readied members. The exact canonical order is given in `SPEC.md`'s STOP table.
- `resolved_versions` includes **path/workspace dependencies** too, each mapped to the target member's declared version (e.g., `api-gateway.core-lib == "1.4.0"`); only external names count toward `summary.total_external_dependencies`.
- In `advisory_matches`, the crate field is named `package` (not `crate_name`).
cargo-workspace-dependency-auditor.solution_solve_sh:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
cd /app

# Compile the canonical Go reference implementation into a self-contained
# binary, then run it. `go run` is avoided because it leaves a cache in
# /root/.cache/go-build which fails on read-only filesystems.
GOCACHE="$(mktemp -d)" GOPATH="$(mktemp -d)" \
    go build -o /tmp/cargo_audit /solution/solve.go
/tmp/cargo_audit

FROM golang:1.24-bookworm@sha256:1a6d4452c65dea36aac2e2d606b01b4a029ec90cc1ae53890540ce6173ea77ac

WORKDIR /app

COPY cgroup_hierarchy/ /app/cgroup_hierarchy/

RUN chmod -R a-w /app/cgroup_hierarchy/

# Verifier dependencies: tmux/asciinema for agent runtime, python3+pytest for tests
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        tmux \
        asciinema \
        python3 \
        python3-pip \
    && (python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages --retries 5 --timeout 120 \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5 \
        || python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --retries 5 --timeout 120 \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5) \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
cgroup-resource-cascade-auditor.instruction_md:
The input directory `/app/cgroup_hierarchy/` contains a snapshot of a Linux cgroup v2 hierarchy. The primary data files are `topology.json` (tree structure with declared CPU, memory, and IO limits), `processes.json` (process-to-cgroup assignments with resource usage and OOM score adjustments), `pressure_events.json` (memory pressure, CPU throttle, and IO latency events with scope fields), `weight_policies.json` (distribution policies with scope and priority boost), `oom_config.json` (OOM kill parameters and thresholds), `config.json` (audit thresholds and decay factors), `throttle_history.json` (historical throttle data), `cpu_accounting.json` (per-cgroup CPU usage), `memory_stats.json` (memory breakdown), `io_stats.json` (IO byte counts), and `memory_protection.json` (memory.min/low settings). The following files are decorative and should be ignored: `kernel_version.json`, `mount_info.json`, `numa_topology.json`, `cpuset_config.json`, `pids_config.json`, `freezer_state.json`, `hugetlb_config.json`, `rdma_config.json`, `migration_log.json`, `audit_metadata.json`, `systemd_unit_map.json`, `interface_stats.json`, `swap_config.json`.

Implement an auditor that analyzes the cgroup v2 hierarchy and writes a JSON report to `/app/cgroup_hierarchy_report.json`. The implementation must conform to `/app/cgroup_hierarchy/SPEC.md`, which is the authoritative and complete reference for all behavioral rules, edge cases, field formats, and the full output schema.

The output report must contain exactly 8 top-level keys: `effective_limits`, `cpu_distribution`, `overcommit_analysis`, `pressure_responses`, `oom_kill_log`, `post_oom_state`, `throttle_chains`, and `summary`. The JSON must use 2-space indentation with all keys sorted recursively at every nesting level and no trailing newline.

FROM golang:1.24-bookworm@sha256:1a6d4452c65dea36aac2e2d606b01b4a029ec90cc1ae53890540ce6173ea77ac

WORKDIR /app

COPY coredump/ /app/coredump/

RUN chmod -R a-w /app/coredump/

# Verifier dependencies: tmux/asciinema for agent runtime, python3+pytest for tests
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        tmux \
        asciinema \
        python3 \
        python3-pip \
    && (python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages --retries 5 --timeout 120 \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5 \
        || python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --retries 5 --timeout 120 \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5) \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
core-dump-analyzer.instruction_md:
The `/app/coredump/` directory contains a simplified core dump dataset from a crashed multi-threaded server process. Core data files include `process_info.json` (process metadata and crash reason), `memory_mappings.json` (virtual address space layout with ASLR load offsets), `symbols.json` (per-library symbol tables), `heap_allocations.json` (heap allocation records with active/freed state), `signal_handlers.json` (registered signal handlers), `signals.json` (signal definitions), `config.json` (analysis parameters), and eight per-thread files (`thread_001.json` through `thread_008.json`) each containing register snapshots, stack frames, and canary integrity flags. The files `build_metadata.json`, `kernel_info.json`, `crash_notes.json`, `hardware_info.json`, `environment_vars.json`, `file_descriptors.json`, `library_versions.json`, and `debug_sections.json` are decorative and should be ignored.

Implement a core dump analyzer that processes all thread dumps, memory mappings, symbol tables, and heap allocations, then writes a JSON report to `/app/coredump_report.json`. The implementation must conform to `/app/coredump/SPEC.md`, which is the authoritative and complete reference for all behavioral rules, edge cases, field formats, and the full output schema. Read the `STOP — Read These High-Impact Clarifications First` section before implementing, especially the required `phantom_frame_analysis` fields and copied-vs-computed hex formatting rules.

The output report must contain exactly eight top-level keys: `corruption_analysis`, `library_attribution`, `phantom_frame_analysis`, `signal_analysis`, `stack_traces`, `summary`, `symbol_resolution`, and `thread_classification`. The JSON must use 2-space indentation with all keys sorted recursively at every nesting level, and the file must end with `}` as the very last character with no trailing newline.

memory-leak-profiler.environment_Dockerfile:
FROM python:3.11-slim@sha256:2c285c669cc837aa3bcf1af23ea1932b7b5214f9c9d3aad22417446ad91cb4fb
WORKDIR /app

COPY alloc_logs/ /app/alloc_logs/

RUN chmod -R a-w /app/alloc_logs/

# Verifier dependencies: tmux/asciinema for agent runtime, python3+pytest for tests
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        tmux \
        asciinema \
        python3 \
        python3-pip \
    && (python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5 \
        || python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5) \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
memory-leak-profiler.instruction_md:
The input directory `/app/alloc_logs/` contains memory allocation profiling data from a runtime session. The primary data files are `allocations.json` (all heap allocation records with addresses, sizes, owners, and scope levels), `frees.json` (explicit and deferred free operations, some with conditional dependencies), `references.json` (pointer references between allocations including strong and weak types), `transfers.json` (ownership transfer events between modules), `scope_exits.json` (scope exit events that trigger deferred frees), and `config.json` (root set definitions, analysis parameters, decay configuration, and isolation strengthening flags). The `owners/` subdirectory contains per-module metadata files that are informational only. The files `profiler_meta.json`, `session_info.json`, and `heap_params.json` are decorative metadata and should be ignored by the analysis.

Implement a memory leak profiler that analyzes all allocation records, computes ownership transfers, resolves deferred frees (including conditional dependencies), evaluates cross-owner reference decay, determines weak reference strengthening under isolation, performs reachability analysis from root allocations, detects phantom roots, identifies leaked memory components, and detects strong reference cycles (including those formed by strengthened-weak edges), then writes a JSON report to `/app/alloc_logs_report.json`. The implementation must conform to `/app/alloc_logs/SPEC.md`, which is the authoritative and complete reference for all behavioral rules, edge cases, field formats, and the full output schema.

The output report must contain exactly these top-level keys: `allocation_status`, `deferred_free_log`, `leak_chains`, `leak_summary`, `ownership_graph`, `phantom_roots`, `reachability_chains`, and `strong_cycles`. The JSON must use 2-space indentation, recursive alphabetical key sorting at every nesting level, and no trailing newline. Refer to `SPEC.md` for the complete schema and behavioral rules for each field, including Rule 4b (cross-owner reference decay), Rule 4c (weak reference strengthening under isolation), Rule 9 (cyclic-strong-reference detection with strengthened-weak edges), and the conditional deferred free dependency mechanism in Rule 3.

pkt-frag-audit.environment_Dockerfile:
FROM debian:bookworm-slim@sha256:67b30a61dc87758f0caf819646104f29ecbda97d920aaf5edc834128ac8493d3

WORKDIR /app

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        gcc=4:12.2.0-3 \
        libc6-dev \
        libcjson1 \
        libcjson-dev \
        make=4.3-4.1 \
        file \
        tmux=3.3a-3 \
        asciinema=2.2.0-1 \
        python3=3.11.2-1+b1 \
        python3-pip=23.0.1+dfsg-1 \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages \
            pytest==8.4.1 pytest-json-ctrf==0.3.5 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY packet_data/ /app/packet_data/

RUN chmod -R a-w /app/packet_data/
pkt-frag-audit.instruction_md:
The directory `/app/packet_data/` contains IP packet fragmentation data for 15 network flows. Each flow is defined in `flows/<flow_id>.json` and contains an ordered list of received fragments with their byte offsets, lengths, TTL values, arrival timestamps, and checksum validity. Network segment definitions in `segments/<segment_id>.json` specify per-segment MTU caps and congestion levels. Global reassembly parameters (timeouts, thresholds, duplicate limits, overlap policies) are in `config.json`. The files `capture_metadata.json`, `network_topology.json`, `firmware_info.json`, and `policy_notes.json` are informational metadata and should be ignored.

Implement the auditor in C — compile with `gcc` and run the resulting binary to produce a JSON report at `/app/reassembly_report.json`. No interpreter-based scripts (Python, shell, Ruby, etc.) may remain in `/app`, `/tmp`, `/var/tmp`, or `/root` after the report is produced. The implementation must conform to `/app/packet_data/SPEC.md`, which is the authoritative and complete reference for all behavioral rules, edge cases, field formats, and the full output schema.

The output report must contain exactly these seven top-level keys: `anomaly_summary`, `flow_details`, `overlap_analysis`, `reassembly_results`, `segment_statistics`, `summary`, and `timeout_analysis`. The report must use 2-space indentation, recursively sorted keys at every nesting level, and must not end with a trailing newline.
pkt-frag-audit.solution_solve_sh:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
cd /app
gcc -std=c99 -O2 -o /app/pkt_frag_audit /solution/auditor.c -lcjson -lm
rm -f /app/reassembly_report.json
/app/pkt_frag_audit
echo "Reassembly report generated successfully."
